## [00:00 - 03:03]

now go down a level and look at the different scaling methods that we use within the BPM method. Now we've talked about scale versus growth, we've talked about the different ways that we scale up using the GTCBO method. Now this part is looking at the different types of scaling. Now you'll know that there is only one way of scaling which is to spend more money on your Ad accounts, right? If you want to increase profitability you can do that without spending more but when we talk about scaling generally we're talking about the ability to spend more and maintain or improve our profitability. Do bear in mind that when you do scale up there will be times where your return on ad spend is not as good as it has been. So it's really important just before you go deep into scaling that you do understand this happens. The reason why it happens is Facebook is trying to speculate, it's trying to find relevant people based on what you've done in the past and this is why I like to spend time optimizing and building stability because whilst I'm doing that I'm filling the ad account, the campaigns, the adsets and ads with good quality data. Facebook needs this data to build a model of who else to go after. So if you're currently spending whatever you know $100 a day $1000 a day etc and you want to scale up the data that your pixel has collected into your ad account, into your campaign, into your ad set and into your ad that goes a long way to determining how successful you will be on scaling. So when I go through the graduation testing process and I'm trying to find my winning ads and audiences and then I go through stabilization and optimization, yes I'm trying to get my stable results and I'm trying to squeeze out more revenue, profitability etc but I'm also building the data inside of my ad account so that when I'm ready for scaling that data is set. So when you go and spend more money, so let's say for example you want to go from $1000 a day to $3000 and you want to do it in let's say three four days time. There are different ways you might want to do that but if you've spent let's say a week, two weeks giving Facebook good quality data and getting good scores in the auction, so do go through the auction insights and the auction part of the optimization training then Facebook will store that in your campaigns and in your ad sets and in your ads. So when you start to increase budget especially if you've got enough data then Facebook will know who to go for to try and find more people similar to the ones that were already purchased because you know so it's a game of signals. The more you spend the more you're telling Facebook I'm happy the less you spend or you know the more you decrease your budgets you're telling Facebook you're unhappy. So when it comes to scaling if you're pushing up that

## [03:03 - 06:08]

spend yet you're not hitting your targets just think about that for a second. You're increasing your spends but you're not happy with performance yet you still keep increasing and optimizing and tweaking in the hope that results will come back. You're telling Facebook that you're happy with results and that's why we use strategies to train trim bad performing ads for example or punish the auction by reducing the budget. So those optimization measures that we've been through in the previous module that all applies when we're going through scaling as well because we want to train the auction train the machine because this is all code right there's inputs there's outputs we've been through the BPM method and really gone through the early part around the brand driven aspect of it about understanding your avatar your funnels etc this is now a game of data. So when it comes to scaling you want to make sure that the data Facebook has is as strong as it can be then you know there's different ways that you can increase the budget but ultimately it comes down to these two factors V and H scaling. V is vertical scaling which is taking your existing campaigns and increasing the budget or horizontal scaling which is duplicating your campaigns or your ad sets or introducing new campaigns like this is the other thing about scaling is it's not just about you know I found my winning audiences and my ads and I'm just going to push that as far as I can. You want to de-risk having too few campaigns but also you don't want to create too many that it becomes unstable as well because you know I've seen many ad accounts where they have 20, 30, 40 different campaigns running different audiences overlapping etc and they're getting yo-yo performance every single day and just by trimming and consolidating those campaigns down to a more manageable number can actually make a big difference and then working on tweaking and optimizing those as well as increasing the budget. Now two examples I want to share here are of V scaling and H scaling and the different impact of both. On the left hand side we have a V scaling example where we have a budget of $500 a day and we're increasing it by 20% at a time so 500 to 600, 600 to 720, 720 etc etc it's a compound effect. What that means is when I apply my 20% increase to that $500 and now I'm spending $600 a day if I now apply 20% on top of that it's applied on the 600 so if you look at the 500 to 600 increase that's $100 from 600 to 720 that's $120 so although some people look at 20% budget increases as yeah you know it's not a lot well actually it can be so you know this example here is actually one that we did in a day so you look at for example the question of I want to double my spend in a day how can I do it using

CBO because CBO deals with this so much better than the kind of ad set style of setting budgets we were able to do this but it was after we spent weeks building the stability with optimized etc etc ## [09:11 - 10:08]

automation that will do this without me even monitoring and it will make the right decisions because I've spent the time to apply these rules myself and then I've written them down and then I've automated them so I can go from $500 to $1000 quite quickly and ad spend without doing anything and that's the beauty of this that when once you understand the whole process of the bpm method it's to is to release you from ads manager I don't want to spend all my time in ads manager I do want to spend my time working on campaigns and promotions and new videos and new landing page ideas and funnels and things like that because the scaling of facebook ads comes from you not from facebook it comes from your intelligence and what you put in so this this section of the different scaling strategies will also depend on what you're looking for fast scaling or growth and then how to apply that for your business as well
